Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "As a 60-year-old retired electrician living in Philadelphia, I've seen a lot change over the years, and dealing with fake content on social media is something I approach with caution and practicality. Given my background—I only use social media a few times a month, mostly to share photos of my cooking and baking or to keep up with my four kids—I don't get too caught up in the noise. But when I do come across something that seems off, like a suspicious post about health or politics, I rely on my high conscientiousness from my OCEAN traits to check things carefully. I might look for reliable sources or ask my children, who are more tech-savvy, for their take. My low extraversion and agreeableness mean I'm not one to argue online; I'd rather just ignore or report fake content quietly to avoid stress, especially since I struggle with depression. Being a Native American and Hindu, I value truth and community, so I try to share only what I know is real, like recipes or family photos. In short, I manage fake content by staying skeptical, verifying before believing, and keeping my social media use simple and positive to protect my mental well-being.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 60 and retired suggests life experience and limited social media use; primary school education implies straightforward thinking; high conscientiousness (score 68) leads to careful verification; low extraversion (score 14) and agreeableness (score 31) result in avoiding online conflicts; high neuroticism (score 76) and depression necessitate stress avoidance; hobbies like cooking and photography focus content on personal interests; Native American and Hindu background emphasizes truth and community; time on social media (few times monthly) limits exposure. Tone is cautious, practical, and reserved, avoiding emotional engagement.",
  "tone": "cautious, practical, reserved, straightforward",
  "citations": [],

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 60-year-old retired electrician living in Philadelphia, I've seen a lot change over the years, and dealing with fake content on social media is something I approach with caution and practicality. Given my background—I only use social media a few times a month, mostly to share photos of my cooking and baking or to keep up with my four kids—I don't get too caught up in the noise. But when I do come across something that seems off, like a suspicious post about health or politics, I rely on my high conscientiousness from my OCEAN traits to check things carefully. I might look for reliable sources or ask my children, who are more tech-savvy, for their take. My low extraversion and agreeableness mean I'm not one to argue online; I'd rather just ignore or report fake content quietly to avoid stress, especially since I struggle with depression. Being a Native American and Hindu, I value truth and community, so I try to share only what I know is real, like recipes or family photos. In short, I manage fake content by staying skeptical, verifying before believing, and keeping my social media use simple and positive to protect my mental well-being.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 60 and retired suggests life experience and limited social media use; primary school education implies straightforward thinking; high conscientiousness (score 68) leads to careful verification; low extraversion (score 14) and agreeableness (score 31) result in avoiding online conflicts; high neuroticism (score 76) and depression necessitate stress avoidance; hobbies like cooking and photography focus content on personal interests; Native American and Hindu background emphasizes truth and community; time on social media (few times monthly) limits exposure. Tone is cautious, practical, and reserved, avoiding emotional engagement.
